▲ Page top
Great for wis hybrids.
QuoteReply
# Jan 11 2002 at 5:19 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591765525919
Scholar
748 posts
Just as sapphire items (int/str) are good for int based hybrids (bards and shadowknights, but especially shadowknights), ruby tmes are great for wis based hybrids (beastlords, rangers, and palladins) who need both the str for their melee abilities and wis for mana. I'd imagine shamans, druids, and clerics might want these too, as it can't hurt to be both stronger and wiser, but at a certain point they don't get into melee fighting that much and will concentrate mostly on wis gear. But hybrids will always need both.
▲ Page top
RE: Great for wis hybrids.
QuoteReply
# Jan 26 2002 at 11:49 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592251515951
281 posts
eh zar u missed the fact of ac...any melee char (hybrids) should talk ac over stats :( i kinda leanred this the hard way tonight when i was fighting Vluudeen (big named wurm in GD) i woulda traded in all my stats for more ac :P she/he/it was double whackin me in the 100's and fast...not a nice feeling
▲ Page top
RE: Great for wis hybrids.
QuoteReply
# Jan 26 2002 at 11:52 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592251515951
281 posts
oh btw...forgot, that +4 wis to hybrids is nothing...it only adds up to a total of a few mana points :( would be most helpful for trade skills though

Hi,

▲ Page top
Price
QuoteReply
# Aug 15 2001 at 2:08 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592250220775
Scholar
37 posts
Something important to realize when pricing items to sell is the level of the consumers you're trying to reach.

This item has +4 Str/+4 Wis. It's a very good item for any wis-based caster, as Str is always needed, at least for carrying stuff.

However, this item has higher level versions, which means that anyone who wants to shell out serious money for such an item will probably NOT want the Silver version, and would proceed to buy the Platinum or Velium version.

In essence, this means that to sell this item at 300pp is not wise, considering that the ones who will want to buy it are lowbies who need an upgrade, but can't afford the Plat or Velium versions. Therefore, about 220pp is a good choice, as it will give you roughly a 90% profit rating, while staying at a reasonable price for lowbies who wish to buy from you.

Out,
Ellon
21st level Enchanter
Drinal Server
Proud member of the Mystical Titans
▲ Page top

QuoteReply
# Aug 11 2001 at 9:07 PM Rating: Decent

15 posts
Geez i have been selling them for 250pp
▲ Page top
posting here
QuoteReply
# Jul 13 2001 at 10:21 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592344474330
Sage
3,705 posts
If you want to buy one, put it in your want to buy list for your character. don't post here. Those postings have no practical value to people who want to find out about the item.

This is the silver (lower level) version of the player made wisdom veil. It has good stats, but unfortunately no AC (i don't think any of the veils do). The Wis is the critical one for my cleric but +4 strength is also a nice bonus. I got mine from a guild member as a gift, but would certainly have paid the average price listed here. Really would like the platinum version, but this is great for now (level 16 cleric). Gonna upgrade my chest item to make up for lack of ac. If you are just beginning to upgrade your wis, look for earrings and rings first, since you probably don't have armor class item there. All in all, a pretty nice item. BTW the high wis also helps improve your trade skills, like blacksmithing, brewing, etc.
